entry.title: Re: Is there enhancement to stop creating inspection lot or make it "skipped" in case of r
entry.content:
Hi,

 

Since the inspection lot is created during delivery save, raising an error in QPAP_FLEX_PLAN is not suitable (it blocks the transaction).

There is no direct enhancement to stop only the lot creation without affecting the document.

Better options:

  • Control via Inspection Type / QM configuration (recommended first).
  • Handle it in goods movement BADI (eg., MB_DOCUMENT_BADI) and suppress logic based on storage location.
  • Or post-process the lot and set it to "Skipped" after creation.

I suggest checking QM configuration before going for enhancement.

Hope this clarifies,

entry.title: Re: Artificial intelligent
entry.content:
AI in SAP Business One (SAP B1) can play a big role in improving efficiency and decision making.

Some practical areas where AI can help:

  • Sales forecasting - Predict future sales based on historical data
  • Inventory optimization - Suggest optimal stock levels and reduce overstock/shortage.
  • Cash flow prediction - Analyze payment patterns and forecast liquidity.
  • Customer insights - Identify buying behavior and suggest cross-sell/up-sell
  • Automation - Intelligent document processing (invoices, approvals, etc.)

In short, AI in SAP B1 helps smalls and mid-sized businesses move from reactive reporting to predictive and smarter decision- making.

entry.title: Re: AI capabilities for custom Application
entry.content:
Yes, this scenario is possible.

If you are doing a L&S from Web Dynpro to FIORI (list report), you can integrate GenAI capabilities using SAP AI Core / AI Services or via external LLM APIs exposed through BTP.

For your requirement (AI/Joule answering questions related to the report data):

  • Expose the report data via Odata
  • Create a service layer that sends relevant context to the AI model
  • Return AI-generated insights or summaries back to the FIORI UI

If you build it as a RAP-based UI5 application, it is even better. RAP provides:

  • Clean service exposure (OData V4)
  • Better extensibility
  • Easier integration with BTP AI services

Regarding Joule specifically - direct embedding depends on system landscape and SAP's roadmap. But technically, contextual AI assistance on top of your report is absolutely achievable.

In short:

Lift & Shift -> Possible

RAP-based app -> Recommended for better AI integration.

Hope this helps.

entry.title: SAP Health Data Services for FHIR - 2602: Release highlights
entry.content:
Dear Healthcare Community,

I’m excited to share an update on the latest release of SAP Health Data Services for FHIR.

About SAP Health Data Services for FHIR: SAP Health Data Services for FHIR supports you in designing, implementing, and operating an application or service relying on health-related data. At its core, it is a cloud-native implementation of the HL7® FHIR® standard. The service allows the profiling and storing of resources according to your business and localisation needs. It lets you manage health data using provided REST APIs, messaging endpoints and events, and offers extensibility with custom resources and FHIR extensions.

Release cycles: SAP Health Data Services for FHIR follows quarterly release cadence. This ensures you receive regular feature updates and innovations. Our release numbering follows a year-month format—for example, Release 2602 indicates the second month of 2026.

Release highlights: Covers short and simple to understand key features and innovation as part of the release.

I’m Manoj, Product Expert for SAP Healthcare, and I’m here to keep you informed about the latest developments in our healthcare product portfolio.

So, let's look into the highlights of 2602 release for SAP Health Data Services for FHIR.

  • GCP hyper-scaler enablement of SAP Health Data Services for FHIR.
    • Value delivered: Access to SAP Health Data Services for FHIR in all GCP regions on demand, expanding beyond existing AWS infrastructure.
  • European region data processing and access control for SAP Health Data Services for FHIR.
    • What does this mean for you: 
      • Data processing and storage within European Economic Area (EEA) and Switzerland.
      • Exclusive deployment of data centres and services within EEA and Switzerland.
  • SAP Health Data Services for FHIR compliance to SAP cloud events.
    • What you gain:
      • Consistent and unified SAP cloud event format aligned with SAP product ecosystem.
      • Streamline event-driven integration and data interchange capabilities.
      • Built to align with the evolving landscape of Event Driven Architecture (EDA), featuring robust support for open-source Async APIs.
      • Connect effortlessly across diverse technology stacks through comprehensive Async API compatibility.
      • Enable smooth event-based data exchange between SAP systems, third-party applications, and external platforms.

Stay tuned to this space for more such updates.

Manoj Kumar
(Product Expert - Healthcare)

entry.title: Integration Stragety: Integrating SAP SuccessFactors To-Do Items with Third-Party Task Centers
entry.content:
• Background
  • Pain Points
  • Standard Design: ToDoEntryV2 OData API
  • Key Fields
  • Best Practices & Implementation Insights
  • Critical Technical Constraints
  • To-Do Behavior Analysis by Category & Status
  • To-Do Behavior Analysis by User Action
  • To-Do Integration Logic Design
  • 1. Incremental Sync (Frequent Updates)
  • 2. Full Sync (Deletion Identification)
  • Data Flow Overview
  • Conclusion

Background

In a typical enterprise architecture, a centralized To-Do Task Center aggregates actionable items from various systems and assigns them to users. SAP SuccessFactors is a primary source for HR-related tasks, such as performance reviews, job requisition approvals, and leave requests.
To integrate SuccessFactors with a third-party task center, the standard approach is: SuccessFactors OData API. This requires the task center (or middleware) to consume SuccessFactors OData APIs with the scheduled job. While this provides a seamless user experience, it requires technical expertise to handle specific API behaviors.

Pain Points

In past integration projects, customers frequently encounter these challenges:

  1. Lack of Real-Time "Completion" Events: SuccessFactors Intelligent Services Center (ISC) does not support a universal "To-Do Completed" event. Consequently, we must rely on scheduled batch jobs to polling the API, which can lead to delays to reflect the latest status in the third-party system.
  2. Inconsistent Behaviors Across Modules: Modules like Performance, Recruiting, and Employee Central have different task structures and completion/deletion behaviors. This makes a "one-size-fits-all" integration strategy difficult.

Standard Design: ToDoEntryV2 OData API

According to the SAP SuccessFactors API Reference, To-Do items are represented by the ToDoEntryV2 entity.

Key Fields

Property
Description
categoryIdThe category ID (e.g., Performance, Recruiting). Can be used as a filter in the API call.
status0 & 1: Future steps (invisible on UI).
2: Active/Current (visible to user).
3: Completed (visible for 30 days).
4: Soft deleted.
linkUrlThe deep link that takes the user directly to the task in the SuccessFactors UI.
subjectIdThe ID of the related object (e.g., User ID for people-based tasks or Workflow Request ID).
todoEntryIdThe unique business key for the task.
todoEntryNameThe descriptive name of the task in the user's locale.

Best Practices & Implementation Insights

In order to build a robust integration, consider the following best practices:

Critical Technical Constraints

  1. Diverse Completion Logic: For Performance tasks, completion changes the status to 3. However, for Onboarding tasks (e.g., "New Hire Data Review"), completion causes the item to be deleted from the API response entirely. I list a clear table below to summarize the behaviors by module and user action, which is crucial for designing the integration logic.
  2. 30-Day Retention: The API only exposes items with Status=3 (Completed) for 30 days. To maintain a complete historical record, the external task center must persist this data.
  3. Handling Status 1: Some forms (Salary, Performance, Job Reqs) appear in the API with Status=1 before reaching the current approver. These should be filtered out to prevent "ghost" tasks in the third-party system.
  4. ToDoEntryID Re-generation: If a form linked with a todo item is deleted and restored in SuccessFactors, a new todoEntryId is generated.
  5. Link Behavior: For some categories, the linkUrl remains in the API after completion but redirects to the SF Home Page instead of the specific form.

To-Do Behavior Analysis by Category & Status

To design a robust integration, it is critical to analyze how different categories respond via the OData API. The table below details common behaviors observed during implementation and testing.

Category IDProduct AreaBusiness TaskStatus 0/1 (Pending/Future) Status 2 (Current/Active)Status 3 (Completed)Deletion/Disappearance ScenariosBehavior During Status ChangeBehavior After Restore/RestartDeep Link Behavior (Status 3)
12CalibrationComplete CalibrationN/ASession in progressN/A1. Session inactivated
2. Session finalized		2 → Removed: Task is deleted from API once complete.New todoEntryId generated.Link is removed along with the task.
0PerformanceReview PerformanceStatus 1: Task exists but hasn't reached approver.Task reached current approver.Approver completed task.Form deleted via Admin Center / Backend.1 → 2 → 3: Standard workflow.
1,2,3 → Removed if form deleted.		New todoEntryId generated.Link remains; navigates to completed form.
3CompensationPlanning Task ReviewStatus 1: Task exists but hasn't reached approver.Task reached current approver.Approver completed task.Form deleted via Admin Center / Backend.1 → 2 → 3: Standard workflow.
1,2,3 → Removed if form deleted.		New todoEntryId generated.Link remains; navigates to completed form.
61 / 68OnboardingNew Hire Data Review / Rehire VerificationN/ATask reached current approver.N/A1. Task completed (One-time)
2. Onboarding cancelled / No Show.		2 → Removed: Task is deleted from API once complete.New todoEntryId generated.Link is removed along with the task.
53OnboardingNew Hire TasksN/ATask reached current approver.Approver completed task.Onboarding cancelled / No Show.2 → 3: Standard transition.
2,3 → Removed if Onboarding cancelled.		New todoEntryId generated.Link remains; navigates to completed task UI.
5RecruitingApprove Job RequisitionStatus 1: Task exists but hasn't reached approver.Task reached current approver.Approver completed task.1. Form deleted
2. Requisition withdrawn/cancelled.		1 → 2 → 3: Standard workflow.
1,2,3 → Removed if deleted.		New todoEntryId generated.Link remains; navigates to completed form.
10RecruitingProvide Interview FeedbackN/AAssessment pending.Assessment completed.Interviewer record deleted.2 → 3: Standard transition.
2,3 → Removed if record deleted.		New todoEntryId generated.Link remains; navigates to interview summary.
11RecruitingApprove Job OfferStatus 1: Task exists but hasn't reached approver.Task reached current approver.Approver completed task.Form deleted via Admin Center / Backend.1 → 2 → 3: Standard workflow.
1,2,3 → Removed if deleted.		New todoEntryId generated.Link remains; navigates to completed offer.
17EC ManagementApprove Position Change / MDFN/AWorkflow in progress.Workflow completed.N/A (Deletion usually results in status change).2 → 3: Standard workflow transition.New todoEntryId generated.Link remains; navigates to completed workflow.
14EC WorkflowsApprove Employee Central WorkflowsN/AWorkflow in progress.Workflow completed.N/A (Standard Employee Life-cycle workflows).2 → 3: Standard workflow transition.New todoEntryId generated.Link remains; navigates to completed workflow.

To-Do Behavior Analysis by User Action

The following table maps specific business actions to their resulting API status. This logic is essential for the middleware to determine whether to push, update, or remove a task from the third-party system.

Category IDProduct AreaWorkflow Status: Before User Approval Workflow Status: At User's StepWorkflow Status: User Completed / EndWorkflow Status: Sent Back (Returned)Workflow Status: Reassigned / ForwardedWorkflow Status: Withdrawn by InitiatorWorkflow Status: Process/Form Deleted
12CalibrationN/AStatus = 2RemovedRemovedRemovedRemovedN/A
0PerformanceStatus = 0Status = 2Status = 3RemovedRemovedRemovedRemoved
3CompensationStatus = 0Status = 2Status = 3RemovedRemovedRemovedRemoved
61 / 68Onboarding (Admin)N/AStatus = 2RemovedN/ARemovedRemovedRemoved (Cancel Onboarding)
53Onboarding (New Hire)N/AStatus = 2Status = 3N/ARemovedRemovedRemoved (Cancel Onboarding)
5Recruiting (Requisition)N/AStatus = 2Status = 3RemovedRemovedRemovedRemoved
10Recruiting (Interview)N/AStatus = 2Status = 3RemovedRemovedRemovedRemoved
11Recruiting (Offer)N/AStatus = 2Status = 3RemovedRemovedRemovedRemoved
17EC Position / MDFN/AStatus = 2Status = 3Status = 3*Status = 3*Status = 3*N/A (Not supported)
14EC WorkflowsN/AStatus = 2Status = 3Status = 3*Status = 3*Status = 3*N/A (Not supported)

Note: For EC Workflows (14/17), actions like "Sent Back" or "Reassign" often result in the current task record being marked as completed (Status 3) for that specific user, while a new task is generated for the next recipient.*

To-Do Integration Logic Design

Integration Process

To handle these module inconsistencies cases where tasks are either marked as Status 3 or simply removed from the API response, a dual-sync strategy is required.

1. Incremental Sync (Frequent Updates)

  • Frequency: Every 3 minutes.
  • Logic: Fetch to-dos changed in the past hour based on LastModifiedDateTime.
  • Purpose: Captures new tasks and status updates (2 → 3) quickly.

2. Full Sync (Deletion Identification)

  • Frequency: Every 10 minutes.
  • Logic: Fetch the full list of pending to-dos and compare it against the middleware's local database.
  • Deletion Detection: If a task exists in the local database but is no longer returned by the SuccessFactors API (and its status is not 3), the middleware identifies it as Deleted and removes it from the third-party system.

Data Flow Overview

The middleware maintains an SFTo-Do Database to track the relationship between the todoEntryId, UserId, and SubjectId. This allows the system to accurately identify when a task has been reassigned or removed entirely from the source system.

Conclusion

Integrating SuccessFactors To-Do items effectively requires moving beyond simple delta syncs. By implementing a "Full Sync vs. Incremental Sync" strategy and accounting for module-specific deletion behaviors, you can ensure a reliable and robust experience for users within a centralized Task Center. Finally, I also hope that SAP could make to-do item management more consistent across modules and provide better event support in ISC for real-time integration in the future.

entry.title: Connection Error "-4009 - Failed to connect SLD, make sure SLD server is correct and available"
entry.content:
We are unable to connect with B1 with error   "-4009 - Failed to connect SLD, make sure SLD server is correct and available"

On SAP B1 and DTW  program work properly 

we already follow SAP note https://me.sap.com/notes/2029714

Solution 1 

1. The "LicenseServer" value defined in ..\SAP\SAP Business One\Conf\b1-local-machine.xml 
2. The "LicenseServer" value defined in ..\SAP\SAP Business One DI API\Conf\b1-local-machine.xml
3. The "License Server" and "Port Number" defined in SAP Business One Service Manager → Settings
4. The licenseServer field in Integration Framework UI → SLD → B1i Server → Choose Company DB → Connectivity List (Active) 

Solution 2

1. Access SAP Business One System Landscape Directory.
 https://MY_SERVER:40000/ControlCenter
2. If your browser asks for security certification just continue to the Web site.
3. Log on using the default user "B1SiteUser".
4. On the tab Servers and Companies, check the value in the Server Name field.
5. If no entry exists in the Servers section, click Add, and add your server name and IP. Click the entries to ensure that all SBO companies are listed on Companies. => every thing work properly

We have try both IP and server name none of this is success.

entry.title: Re: Bank Statement Reconciliation
entry.content:
Good day @Jessicaaguilar1 

Thank you for your question.

What you described is to be expected in bank statement processing, depending on your posting rule and whether the payment was already posted before the bank statement was reconciled. From your description you already had a vendor payment posted and later you matched it in Manage Bank Statements. That flow is often in two separate processes:

  1. Payment posting/clearing step
  2. Bank statement reconciliation step

For that reason, the bank clearing account can temporarily show both debit and credit open items until you clear the corresponding logic, depending on your setup, either manually or automatically.

See the process description in these Blogs: How Automated Processing Rules for Bank Statement works?, and Future of Electronic Bank Statement Processing in SAP S/4HANA Cloud - Release of 4X8

Question: Why does the item in the clearing account not get automatically cleared when the payment is matched with the bank statement?

Answer: Because reconciling the bank statement item does not always mean automatic clearing of the bank clearing account item in the same step. It depends on factors like:

  • The external transaction à posting rule mapping
  • Whether your process is configured as one or two step
  • Whether your posting rule is designed to post only, it posts and clears
  • Whether you are running a separate automatic clearing job afterward.

Basically saying, Reconciliation in Manage Bank Statements matches the bank movement but cleaning up the clearing account can still require separate clearing logic.

See the following references for more details:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/511decb0856842b682bbd337c81db5ca/60df2bcd2913484a91cddddf5ad7812c.html

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/how-automated-processing-rules-for-bank-statement-works/ba-p/13855860

Question: Is it normal for the clearing account to generate two open items (one debit and one credit) after reconciling a payment that was already posted?

Answer: Yes, this can be normal if you have implemented a two step process as mentioned above. What you can typically see is:

  • One posting comes from the payment
  • Another posting comes from your bank statement posting
  • Then both hit the bank clearing account on opposite sides
  • They remain open until they are cleared against each other

So, seeing one positive and one negative open item in this case is often a sign that the system has created both sides, but the final clearing step has not yet been concluded.

See the following references for more details:

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-sap/future-of-electronic-bank-statement-processing-in-sap-s-4hana-cloud-release/bc-p/13739844

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/how-automated-processing-rules-for-bank-statement-works/ba-p/13855860

Question: Should the automatic clearing of the payment with the invoice be executed before or after the bank reconciliation?

Answer: Usually before, if you are using the standard outgoing payment process e.g., payment run. The reason behind is, the payment clears the vendor open item, depending on your setup, the bank statement then later confirms the bank movement and clear the bank clearing account. However, if your payment was posted differently, timing may in this case vary.

Question: Does automatic clearing always need to be performed, even when bank reconciliation is done in Bank Statement Processing?

Answer: In practice, yes if open items remain. Bank reconciliation in app does not guarantee that, vendor invoice is cleared and bank clearing account is cleared in that same step. If the system leaves open items in the bank clearing account, you still need automatic clearing or manual clearing for exceptions to fully close the process.

You may consider the following checks:

Inspect the postings created by the bank statement item in your Manage Bank Statements App. Open a reconciled item and check posting details, rule used and whether a posting only or posting and clearing was carried out.

In your Display Line Items in General Ledger, check the bank clearing account line items by filtering bank clearing G/L, company code and posting date. Confirm if you have one debit open item, one credit open item and same amount ready for clearing.

Review the posting rule setup in your CBC, check the mapping for external transaction code, posting rule, procedure, whether clearing is expected in the same step or later.

Finally, if your business expects these items to clear automatically, then verify:

  • Whether an automatic clearing job is scheduled
  • Account type scope/company code
  • Whether the bank clearing is included

To summarise:

  • It is normal to see two open items on the bank clearing account after matching the bank statement line, if a vendor payment was already posted before bank statement reconciliation.
  • That usually means the payment posting and bank statement posting were both created, but the final clearing of the bank clearing account has not yet been executed.
  • Check the external transaction to posting rule mapping and whether your process is designed for one or two step posting.
  • Also confirm if automatic clearing job is required afterwards.

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Vibe Coding SAP Digital Manufacturing Custom Plugins, Widgets and Extensions
entry.content:
In this blog post, I will guide you through how one of my vibe coding sessions for SAP Digital Manufacturing plugins, widgets and extensions look like.

Currently, my vibe coding setup looks like this: VSCode running Cline, pointing to SAP AI Core using Anthropic Cloude 4.5 Opus, pushing code to GitHub and deploying to SAP Digital Manufacturing.

In my case, I´m using SAP AI Core for the LLM access, but you can easily point Cline to your LLM of preference by configuring your credentials or API Keys.

First of all, the most important part in my personal opinion, the context. I never start a vibe coding session from scratch, I always start by importing some context files I created over time, which store the knowledge and learnings from the previous vibe coding sessions.

After each vibe coding session, or as soon as "we" learn something new, or solve an issue, I always use a prompt like this: "Now, store /update all this new knowledge and learnings into a file for future reference". With that simple prompt, a markdown file is created by the LLM, storing the most important details and learnings about what was just accomplished, that can be used in future sessions.

Recently, LLM context windows went from 4,000 tokens to over 1,000,000. In plain terms, AI went from reading a sticky note to reading an entire book before answering you, so you can import massive amounts of learnings and knowledge as an input to each new session, to again a rrrrreeally big jump start.

These are some of the files I normally import, for SAP Digital Manufacturing vibe coding. They are very personal, and I will not share them, please don´t ask. 🙂

Once the context is loaded, I write a prompt describing exactly what I want to be coded, including naming conventions, data types, how it should look and feel, which requirements and capabilities it must cover, and any other aspects that come to mind. Honestly, I’m not overly critical at this stage. I treat it as an initial draft based on my vision and intent, knowing that I can continuously refin

As simple as that, immediately after my prompt, it starts working, creating the folder structure and coding the required files:

Even a comprehensive README file is created right away, which is one of the instructions that comes from one of the context files I mentioned about and imported before starting the session.

These are the folder structure and files created:

Which I can just pack into a zip file, to be imported in DM.

In Manage PODs 2.0 app, I just need to open 'Extensions' tab and click 'Upload', to add name, description, namespace and import the source code to be imported:

And that's it! The plugin is imported and is ready for testing.

In Manage PODs 2.0 design time, all I need to do is find the Custom Widget from the 'Plugins' tab and drag and drop to somewhere in the POD, save it and run it for testing.

When testing, it almost worked first time, but it didn't displayed any data:

So I had to do the first round of debugging, the first interaction:

This time, it called the API at the right time to retrieve data, but failed to call the API endpoint successfully:

After some vibe coding debugging interactions, it finally succeeded calling the API properly, data was properly displayed:

From the order level custom fields:

This whole vibe coding session for a fully functional custom widget, including API calls, was ready in under 20 mins.

Check all my custom plugins and widgets and extensions at my GitHub repository.

What do you think about vibe coding custom SAP Digital Manufacturing stuff? Let me know in the comments. 🙂

Stay tuned for more! Thanks,
Manoel Costa

entry.title: Re: Inspection lot deletion
entry.content:
• There is no standard SAP transaction to “restore” a deleted lot.
  •  Possible Recovery / Workarounds
  1. Check Transport/Backup
      • If the deletion was in a test or development system, you may be able to re-import from a backup or transport.
      • In production, Basis can check system backups or database restore points.
  1. Recreate the Inspection Lot
      • For GR-based lots → cancel and re-post the Goods Receipt (MIGO).
      • For production lots → reverse and re-confirm the production order step.
      • For recurring inspection → re-run the scheduling job.
      • Trigger a new inspection lot by repeating the original business process:
      • This generates a fresh inspection lot with a new lot number.
  1. Audit Logs
      • Basis/security can check change logs (SCU3, Security Audit Log) to confirm who deleted the lot and when.
      • This helps in compliance and RCA (root cause analysis).
    • Deleted inspection lots cannot be restored directly.
    • The only practical solution is to recreate the lot by repeating the triggering transaction or restore from a backup if available.

 From a Basis side, the action is irreversible in the database. The best path is either functional recreation (new lot) or technical restore (backup).

There is no standard SAP transaction to “restore” a deleted lot.

Is there a way to automate these anniversary‑based bonus accruals without using Interim Time Account Rules or similar workarounds?

entry.title: Re: Customer Invoices based on batches from Outbound deli...
entry.content:
When a material is batch-managed, SAP automatically creates batch split items under the main delivery item. These sub-items represent the individual batches. During billing, you can decide whether:

  • The main item is billed and batch items are shown with zero quantity.
  • The batch items are billed and the main item is shown with zero quantity.
  • Both main and batch items are displayed with different billing relevance settings.

Flexible Billing: SAP provides functionality called Flexible Billing of Batch Items, which allows you to control whether the batch main item or sub-items carry the billed quantities and values. This gives some flexibility in how invoices are generated. Check this SAP document Flexible Billing of Batch Items 

Coming to your question 'do we have any solution', out-of-the-box, SAP does not allow selective invoicing of only certain batches from a delivery line item while leaving others for later, unless each batch is represented as a separate billing-relevant item. Since your users don’t want one line per batch in the delivery (due to high batch counts), the standard approach won’t directly meet the requirement.

Possible approaches

Custom Logic (BAdI/Enhancement)
Implement a billing-related BAdI (e.g., SD_BILLING) to filter which batch items are picked into the billing document based on user selection. This way, the user can decide which batches to invoice without splitting the delivery line item into multiple billing-relevant items.

Flexible Billing Configuration
Use the Flexible Billing of Batch Items settings to control whether batch sub-items or main items are billed, and explore if customizing can be extended to allow selective batch invoicing.

entry.title: Re: How do get the standard process library for Supply Ch...
entry.content:
SAP does not currently provide a single, publicly available “process library” site for Supply Chain Management (SCM) that is equivalent to the SuccessFactors process library you mentioned. Instead, the standard SCM process documentation is distributed across the SAP Help Portal and related resources.

Where to Find SCM Process Documentation

SAP Help Portal – Supply Chain Management
This is the central entry point for SCM product documentation. It covers:

  • Supply Chain Planning (e.g., IBP, PP/DS, SNP)
  • Supply Chain Logistics (e.g., EWM, TM)
  • Manufacturing (e.g., MES, Digital Manufacturing Cloud)
    Each section links to detailed process descriptions, configuration guides, and integration scenarios. Check this SAP document Supply chain management

entry.title: Re: while billing Packaging Material will create a COPA d...
entry.content:
Normally, no: Packaging materials are often treated as non-revenue items (e.g., free goods, delivery packaging). If they are not relevant for revenue recognition or profitability, they won’t generate a Profitability Segment (PSG) entry in COPA.

Exceptions: If the packaging material is billed (i.e., has a price condition and is revenue-relevant), then it should generate a PSG document during billing, just like any other material.

Key principle: COPA documents are created only for billing items that are revenue-relevant and mapped to a profitability segment.

Configuration checks to identify DEV vs QAS inconsistency

Material Master

  • Material Type: Is the packaging material set as revenue-relevant in QAS but not in DEV?
  • Valuation Class / Account Assignment Group: These influence whether the material posts to COPA.

Billing Document Configuration

  • Item Category: Check if the item category for packaging materials is marked as “relevant for billing” and “relevant for COPA.”
  • Account Assignment Group: Ensure consistency between DEV and QAS.

COPA Settings

  • Value Field Assignment (Costing-based COPA): Verify that the condition types for packaging materials are mapped to COPA value fields.
  • Derivation Rules (Margin Analysis): Check if profitability segment derivation rules are consistent across systems.
  • PA Transfer Structure: Ensure the transfer structure used in billing is identical in DEV and QAS.

Condition Records

  • Pricing Conditions: If the packaging material has a price condition in QAS but not in DEV, COPA will trigger only in QAS.
  • Statistical vs. Non-statistical Conditions: Statistical conditions don’t flow into COPA.

System Settings

  • Activation of COPA: Confirm that both costing-based and margin analysis are active in DEV. Sometimes one client has margin analysis switched off.
  • Integration Settings: Check FI–CO integration for revenue accounts.

entry.title: Re: From Business Objects to Business Flows: The Missing Layer of RAP Documentation
entry.content:
@Mattias ,
I completely agree that a flowchart (that is how I tend to call it) is often missing.
It can add a lot of value and it can be simple, but it can also become more complex with many sidetracks.
References to other flowscharts and resources (such as specifications detailing a specific part) can all exist in this.

It all sounds great in my head and it would be awesome to have this because

  • it simplifies getting started with new changes
  • pinpointing pain points in existing flows
  • gives a view on complexity in the blink of an eye
  • and for sure there are more besides the most valuable that I see for myself 🙂

In reality I also experience that this is mostly missing, and if it exists it gets discontinued once something moves from project mode into maintenance/support mode.

This despite tools like visio or the free drawio making it straightforward to keep something like this.

Documentation remains a challenge, and to be honest I don't know how to get this in a better state simply because there is many people involved and most of them fall outside my personal circle of influence.

Kudos on the nice blogs.

entry.title: Re: Goods Issunce Against Production Order in SAP EWM
entry.content:
Good day @yasirafridi 

Thank you for your question.

No, in Public Cloud you generally cannot force the MF60 staging process to post movement type 313 instead of the standard posting behaviour (which you currently see as 411) through standard configuration.

The reason is your scenario is a specific process integration flow. In Public Cloud, the movement type used in such standard process flows is typically controlled by the app or process logic and you cannot freely customise it.

Also, 313/315 is the classic two step storage location transfer logic, which is different from the standard staging flow you are using. See the following reference for more details: https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-sap/stock-in-transfer-vs-stock-in-transit-where-to-find-it-how-to-remove-it/ba-p/13577561

I suggest you keep the standard EWM staging process and use warehouse execution confirmations, HU/warehouse task status and delivery status monitoring, to control your operational handover, instead of forcing 313.

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Troubleshooting your Master Data replication to SAP Logistics Management
entry.content:
In this blog we will approach the topic of Master Data replication in SAP Logistics Management (LGM hence forth), and to be more specific possible sources of error that may prevent that process from occurring without a hitch.

Master data, i.e products and business partners must mandatorily be replicated from your SAP S/4HANA system into LGM upon initial system setup and then regularly once you are operational (with a frequency that you can freely determine: multiple times per day, once per day, twice per week...whatever is required to accommodate your organisation). The master data used in your sent transactional documents (purchase orders, inbound deliveries, outbound deliveries), must obligatorily exists in LGM, otherwise you will not be able to integrate and process these documents in LGM. So for anyone wanting to gets their hands on using LGM, not being able to take those warehousing and transportation processes for a spin because master data cannot be synced, can be a source of frustration!

As always, before you think of opening an incident with SAP support, please make sure to check your settings and check the SAP documentation first.

 

Troubleshooting errors

Before I go on, I will make the following assumptions:

  • In the app 'Manage System Connections', for the connection type 3001 - SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud, when you perform a 'Check Connection', you are getting a positive/successful response for the 3 replication objects below (if not, go directly to the section 'Before attempting a replication' further down in this page)

  • You have imported the organisational model (SAP Help) and the organisational levels that will be used by your master data are created 
  • You have imported the units of measure (SAP help) and replicated the Code Lists already (SAP help)
  • You have maintained the attributes of your warehouse(s) in the app 'Manage Warehouse Layout' --> Manage Warehouse Attributes.

Error #1 - no filtering criteria maintained.

Whether you are trying to perform a Product or Business Partner replication run, you will need to make a variant selection by either opting to select a catch all 'All Active Variant' option, or opt to indicate a 'Single Active Variant' option.

 However one step that is often missed is that in the definition of the variant(s) no filter criteria have been indicated (How do you think LGM will know what to replicate?). This is not right, replication variants must be fully defined, and must thus contain filtering criteria.

Takeaway --> Make sure at least one filtering logic is specified for each of your selected replication variant(s).

 How the filtering logic works and examples on how to maintain them are provided in the SAP Help here.

Error #2 - Product attributes not maintained or incorrect.

This is specific to the replication of Products. This requirement is clearly indicated in the SAP help in the Product Replication page. Namely, "to be able to replicate products you have to maintain the product number format in the Manage System Connections app. For more information, see Manage System Connections."

In addition, you have to make sure that the settings you indicate here (for the connection type 3001) are identical to what you have defined in your S/4HANA System - this is also echoed in the SAP help in Manage System Connections, where it also states as a cautionary measure "These settings should be configured during the initial setup and should not be modified frequently, as doing so may result in data loss or inconsistencies."

The path to check these settings in your S/4HANA system is SAP Customizing Implementation Guide --> Logistics - General --> Material Master --> Basic Settings --> Define Output Format of Material Numbers. You can also use the transaction code 'OMSL' to reach it directly.

Takeaway --> Make to have maintained the product number format in LGM with settings identical to your S/4HANA system.

 

Error #3 - Missing business partner function

This is specific to the replication of Business Partners and not so much a source of error, but something that might leave you scratching your head asking yourself why you cannot see carriers being replicated, or being able to use a replicated business partner as a carrier in LGM. 

This specific aspect is covered here in the SAP help. Essentially and this is 'hard wired', but for a business partner to be replicated to LGM as a carrier, or to be able to use then as such in LGM, they must correspondingly be defined as carriers in S/4HANA, with a very specific business partner role - with id CRM010. I cannot say  that I have ever seen a system where this business partner role did not exist, but if that is the case for you, then follow the guidance in this SAP help page, to create it in your S/4HANA system (and run the S/4HANA Configuration replication after to make sure it comes across in LGM!).

Takeaway --> Make sure that the business role ID CRM010 (Carrier) exists in your S/4HANA system and make sure to create your -LGM relevant- carriers in S/4HANA with that role also.

 

 

 

Before attempting a replication

Depending on your security/accountability organisational setup, it may be that different specialist were involved in the technical setup of LGM, meaning that multiple individuals did 'their bit', and then handed it over to you to perform the LGM functional configuration per se. It could also of course be that you did it all yourself, but something did not go quite right.

I would also like to point out a link to the SAP help that, explains reasons for and resolutions to connectivity errors you might encounter here.

So before you attempt a replication, check in the app 'Manage Systems Connections' for the connection type '3001 - SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Master Data' that you are able to connect to your S/4HANA backend, by selecting the 'Check Connection' option.

If the response you receive is as below, then do not attempt a replication. Stop and check your settings first. Some settings to check I have indicated below.

 Destination -name- checks

The error message is already giving you some information --> destination..could not be established. The first and easy check you can make is to make sure that the name of the destination that you indicated in the 3001 connection type in LGM strictly matches the name of the destination that you created in the BTP subaccount where LGM was deployed.

Takeaway --> The name of the destination in the 3001 connection type must match the name of the destination pointing to your S/4HANA system that you created in the LGM BTP subaccount.

Also check to make sure that you have maintained the additional required properties (SAP help). 

If you are in the BTP destination, you might as well to a connectivity check there to make sure the URL you indicated allows you to reach the S/4HANA system (via the cloud connector). A positive response here will be synonymous of a valid Cloud Connector setup. 

Authenticating user checks (authentication failure, cannot access destination)

  • Make sure the user and password you indicated in the BTP destination (above) are correct - beware of typos!
  • Make sure the user you indicated in the BTP destination (above) is not locked in the S/4HANA system (have there already been too many failed attempts?) - check the Logon Data tab in the user record in S/4HANA (Transaction SU01).
  • Make sure the user you indicated in the BTP destination has all the required accesses and authorisations to access the needed Odata API's as described here in the SAP help. As a prerequisite check:
    • Pay attention to the Odata Services you must activate and their type. Business Partner is an Odata V2 API, and Product is an Odata V4 API
    • In case it needs to be said..make sure you have activated the Odata Services in the first place.

 

If you come other errors (and how you resolved them!), feel free to share them in the comment, so that the entire community can benefit from shared knowledge.

entry.title: Re: how to display an error message if goal plan weight i...
entry.content:
Hi user116,

We cannot restrict the user directly in the Goal Plan when the maximum goal weight is reached. Even if an error message is shown, the user is still able to save the goal.

However, we can control this through the Performance Form in the Goal section. By adding the required code in the XML and defining the maximum total goal weight, the system will validate the limit. If the total weight exceeds the defined maximum, the system will not allow the user to move the form to the next step and an error message will be displayed.

If you have any doubts regarding this, please refer to the relevant KBA, where it is explained clearly in a detailed manner.

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2510851

entry.title: BEHAVIOR_ILLEGAL_STATEMENT error raising when Click Send Mail action but Mail can send
entry.content:
Dear All,

We have implemented a custom RAP action sendMail for business object ZI_PACKINGLISTAPP in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud. The logic is written in a custom class under Source Code Library and is executed from the RAP behavior. The action reads selected records using READ ENTITIES, creates an email using CL_BCS_MAIL_MESSAGE, sets recipient, subject, and HTML body, and then sends the email using lo_mail->send(). However, when the action is triggered from the Fiori app, the OData request fails with runtime error BEHAVIOR_ILLEGAL_STATEMENT althouthg the email is  sent. We would like to understand why this logic is not working in the RAP context and which part of the implementation is considered illegal by the RAP framework.

 

Below is the implemented logic for reference:

METHOD sendMail.
  READ ENTITIES OF zi_packinglistapp IN LOCAL MODE
    ENTITY PackingListApp
    ALL FIELDS WITH CORRESPONDING #( keys )
    RESULT DATA(lt_results).

  LOOP AT lt_results ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<fs_result>).
    DATA(lo_mail) = cl_bcs_mail_message=>create_instance( ).
    lo_mail->add_recipient( 'xxx@company.com' ).
    lo_mail->set_subject( 'Status Updated' ).
    lo_mail->set_main(
      cl_bcs_mail_textpart=>create_instance(
        iv_content = 'Notice Item ' &&
<fs_result>-OutboundDeliveryID &&  ' updated. '
        iv_content_type = 'text/html'
      )
    ).
    lo_mail->send( ).
  ENDLOOP.
ENDMETHOD.

Please clarify why this implementation results in BEHAVIOR_ILLEGAL_STATEMENT in S/4HANA Public Cloud RAP, and advise the correct SAP-recommended approach for sending emails (and adding attachments) triggered from a RAP action.

Thanks

entry.title: SAP User Experience Q1/2026 Update – Part 6: SAP Fieldglass
entry.content:
This sixth post in my series gives an overview of latest UX innovations in SAP Fieldglass, for managing your external workforce. The design of the supplier home page has been updated, and a number of further design updates have been made for a consistent SAP Business Suite experience. On-boarding is now made easy with WalkMe. Joule supports further use cases now, and also supports multiple languages, not just English. We have some nice embedded AI use cases, creating a better user experience, as well as many improvements in details that make a difference in day-to-day work.

In case you missed it, Part 1 provides general updates about Joule, SAP Work Zone and SAP Mobile Start.

I’ve grouped the UX innovations into these main sections:

  • Updated Supplier Home Page.
  • Design Updates for Suite Consistency.
  • Joule Innovotions.
  • Embedded AI for improved UX.
  • WalkMe for Easy On-Boarding.
  • Selected Usability Improvements in Applications
  • Further Information

 

Redesigned Supplier Home Page

The supplier home page is the first place that suppliers go to when using SAP Fieldglass, and hence it is crucial that it provides the most important information, actions and navigation options. Based on thorough user research, we have now redesigned the page to deliver a more streamlined and user-centered experience. At the same time, the new design also supports our focus on providing a consistent user experience across all products within the SAP Business Suite, as described in the next section.

Figure 1 shows an example. As you can see, the Announcement card is prominently visible. Users can choose to close it, in which case a button appears allowing them to re-open. The short video in Figure 2 shows what this looks like on a different example page.

You can configure the layout of the redesigned home page for each role with the Layout Manager, which was introduced in the May 2025 release. This learning journey tells you how to do it.

Figure 1: The redesigned supplier home page in SAP Fieldglass. Alt Text: Screen image showing a welcome banner at the top, and a collapsed vertical navigation bar running down the left side. The main screen has a section with pinned links, below which a selection of various cards are shown such as a list of My Work Items, Active Job Postings, Announcements. Two cards have an illustrated message that they have no data to display.

Figure 2: Hiding and re-opening the Announcement card. Alt Text: a short video showing the top half of the home page and the user clicking on “Close” and then “View Announcement”.

Find out more:

  • Smooth and Superior: The New, User-Centered Supplier Home Page.
  • Documentation: Redesigned Supplier Home Page.

 

Design Updates for Suite Consistency

We have made design improvements which are relevant for a consistent user experience across the entire SAP Business Suite, and which are now also available with SAP Fieldglass. As I mentioned in my April blog post Upcoming Design Updates for the SAP Business Suite (SAP Fiori for Web), consistency of the user experience across the various applications used by a user is an important part of overall usability, helping users to feel confident and at ease when using a suite of products – no unexpected surprises.

In this section, we’ll look at these updates (the first two cover the design updates for consistency):

  • Shell bar, navigation menu, user menu.
  • Redesigned user settings.
  • Design updates for more consistent applications.

Shell bar, navigation menu, user menu

Figure 1 above already features the new shell bar, with the collapsed side navigation bar on the left, showing a vertical list of icons representing menu entries. Users can expand the menu to better understand what each menu icon represents, as shown in Figure 3. This is done by clicking on the icon in the top left corner to the left of the SAP logo. It stays expanded until the user closes it, by clicking on the same icon again.

Figure 3 also shows the redesigned user menu, which follows the design for all SAP Business Suite products.

Figure 3: Navigation menu expanded, new user menu. Alt Text: screenshot of the home pages with the navigation menu on the left showing titles next to the icons, the user menu showing two entries “Settings” and “Legal”, and a separate “Sign Out” option at the bottom.

Redesigned User Settings

Selecting Settings in the user menu shown in Figure 3 above brings you to the redesigned settings page, shown in Figure 4 below. This design follows the SAP Fiori design guidelines, thus providing additional UX consistency across the suite. Beyond these visual aspects, it consolidates fields from the My Profile page and the My Preferences page into a single unified Settings page, which is easily accessed via the user menu. Some of the categories have been renamed too. Find out more.

  • Documentation: Updates to Settings Page.

Figure 4: Redesigned and consolidated user settings. Alt Text: Screenshot showing setttings, with full width cards “User Information” and “Appearance”, each with an “Edit” button top right.

Design Updates for More Consistent Applications.

SAP Fieldglass has also introduced the new illustrations from the SAP Design System (SAP Fiori). You can see some examples in Figures 1 and 2 above.

In addition to that, we have also aligned the design of individual applications with the design system. For example, the decision form list view now has the filters at the top of the page, and the Apply Filter button has been renamed to Go, thus aligning terminology – see Figure 5.

Figure 5: Decision form list view filters and Go button. Alt Text: Screen snippet showing “Decision Forms” above two rows of six and three filters. On the far right a highlighted “Go” button.

We have also moved the icons for table actions to the top of the decision form table, again for more consistency. Find out more in the documentation:

  • Decision Form List View UI Updates.

We have made similar updates in other applications, such as the project list view. You can find the full list in the What’s New documentaiton:

  • Project List View User Interface Updates
  • What’s New Viewer for SAP Fieldglass.

 

Joule innovotions

Now, you can use Joule:

  • in many languages, not just English;
  • to recommend job posting templates when create job postings;
  • to approve decision forms, including legal entity recognition.

Joule: Multi-Language Support

You can use Joule in all the languages available in SAP Fieldglass execept for Mongolian, which is currently not available in Joule. Figure 6 shows an example using French, which is used in many countries, not just France.

Figure 6: Joule multi-language support, showing an example in French. Alt Text: Three Joule images starting with a list of suggested prompts, the user chose “Créer une offre d’emploi”, then showing how Joule asks for details, the user entered “chef de projet”, Joule offered three templates, the user chose one and Joule shows the details. In the background a screenshot showing user preferences with the language French selected.

Not only can you interact with Joule in your native tongue, you can also use Joule to find job posting templates and statement of work templates that your company has translated into your language.

For companies that translate job postings and statement of work templates into multiple languages, Joule can automatically detect the user's preferred language and retrieve the corresponding localized template, if available. If not available in the user's preferred language, templates in the company-defined language will be returned. Find out more:

  • Documentation: Translated Template Retrieval in Joule.

Joule: Job Posting Template Recommendations

If you need to create a job posting, Joule can make this easier for you: after stating your requirements for the job, Joule will recommend the top three job posting templates that match best. Figure 7 shows an example where the user has asked for a “Project Manager with 3+ years experience”. In this example, Joule is in full-screen mode, giving space for details of a selected template to be shown on the right. The example shows that the user has clicked on the first suggestion in the list on the left.

Figure 7: Using Joule for SAP Fielglass to create a job posting for a contingent worker, with Joule providing top 3 matching job posting templates. Alt Text: Screenshot of Joule in full-screen mode, with prompts and outcome as described in the text.

Joule: Legal Entity Recognition

If Legal Entity functionality has been enabled in your SAP Fieldglass system, so that you can manage the external workforce for more than one company, you can now specify the legal entity as part of your requirement to Joule when creating job postings or statements of work. Figure 8 shows an example for creating a job posting for the legal entity “UK”.

Figure 8: Legal Entity Recognition by Joule for SAP Fieldglass when creating a job posting. Alt Text: Four Joule images, from left to right, with prompt “Create Job Posting”, Joule asks for details, then “programmer, legal entity is UK”, Joule shows 3 templates, user chose one, Joule asks to confirm, user says “yes”, Joule shows details for reviewing before creating.

Read more:

  • Documentation: Legal Entity Recognition in Joule.

Joule: Decision Form Approvals

When asking Joule for pending work items, Joule will now include Decision Form approvals too, as shown in Figure 9.

Figure 9: Joule for approving Decision Forms. Alt Text: Five Joule screenshots, user chooses “View my pending items”, Joule shows one “Contractor Vetting” pending approval, user selects “Approve Decision Form” from the context menu, Joule asks for a comment, user says “no comment”, decision form is approved.

More Information on Joule

Read this blog post and watch the video to learn more:

  • What’s New for Joule in SAP Fieldglass – November 2025.
  • Video: Introducing Joule in SAP Fieldglass (3:30 minutes).
  • Video: SAP Fieldglass 2025.11 Release Highlights Video - Joule (5:17 minutes).

 

Embedded AI for improved UX

AI-Assisted Skills-Based Hiring

When looking to hire consultants or contingent workers, you want to ensure that you get people with the right skills. Now, SAP Fieldglass makes this easy, with AI assistance.

AI helps you to specify which qualifications to ask for when creating a new job posting: AI-powered qualification recommendations uses AI to analyze your job title and description and then suggests relevant skills. The screen on the left in Figure 9 shows what this looks like.

AI helps you to consolidate qualifications that have different names for the same qualification. Suppliers describe the skills of their employees themselves, leading to a many different names for essentially the same skill. For example, “Azure Cloud,” “Azure”, and “MS Azure” could be used for someone with skills with Microsoft Azure Cloud. With the help of AI, these qualifications can be consolidated into one unique qualification. The screen top right of Figure 10 shows an example of how this is done.

Once skills have unique names, the other aspect that needs to be harmonized is the rating scale: some suppliers use 1 to 5, others perhaps 1 to 6 or 1 to 10. SAP Fieldglass normalizes these.

Having created a posting with appropriate skills, SAP Fieldglass helps you select candidates by making proposals based on the skills (and their ratings) that match, in addition to proposing candidates that have the appropriate job code. The image in Figure 10, bottom right, shows an example.

Figure 10: AI-powered qualification recommendations, qualification consolidation and skill-based candidate proposals in SAP Fieldglass. Alt Text: Three screen snippets. On the left a list of 6 recommended qualifications for skills such as Excel, Powerpoint, Word, with a relevancy shown as a percentage (55%, 50%, …). Top right a list showing Qualification “Microsoft Azure” as heading, with two entries below “Azure Cloud” and “MS Azure” and buttons “Consolidate Qualifications” and “Cancel”. Bottom right a list of 4 potential candidates, their names greyed out, with the qualification matching percentage shown in the far right column.

Read more in this blog post:

  • Smarter Skills-Based Hiring with SAP Fieldglass

Automated Approvals for Resource Time Sheets

Another example where AI can make users much happier is approving resource time sheets. In industries that have high volumes of worker time submissions, manually and dealing with discrepencies in the submitted time sheets can be tedious and time consuming. Now, you can define tolerance limits so that hours that fall within the defined tolerances are automatically approved. This reduces manual effort significantly, freeing up users to do more value-adding activities.

Read more about it:

  • Documentation: Automated Approvals for Resource Time sheets Leveraging Tolerance Limits.

 

WalkMe for Easy On-Boarding

First-time users can often feel a bit lost when using new business software, no matter how easy it is to use – simply the fact that it is unfamiliar can make people feel uncomfortable.

This is where WalkMe comes in, helping users discover how to use the product. SAP Fieldglass now provides WalkMe content out of the box. Figure 11 shows how users can choose from a list of topics. Figure 12 shows how it looks like if a user starts a topic (the welcome pop-over shown on the left), and how WalkMe gives users guidance for each step in the process (the image on the right).

Figure 11: Starting WalkMe in SAP Fieldglass. Alt Text: Screenshot of the home page with a full-height pop-over on the right with heading “Type in your question” above a text entry field, below that sections “General”, “Contingent Workforce”, “Services” with a list of entries such as “Review work items” or “Create a job posting”.

Figure 12: WalkMe examples in SAP Fieldglass. Alt Text: Two screenshots with pop-overs. On the left: “Review work items” with a description and a “Guide me” button; on the right “Approve Time Sheet” pop-over with a small popover connected to the “Approve” button, explaining what to do.

Find out more:

  • Documentation: WalkMe Walkthroughs Available in SAP Fieldglass.

 

Selected Usability Improvements in Applications

Applications in SAP Fieldglass are constantly improving, in many cases not just with new features and functions but also with aspects that improve the overall user experience. Even if they are only improvements in details: the details matter if you want to provide a good experience, since if you get them wrong, they can be very annoying.

Below are some highlights for these improvements in details.

Reusable Resource Types for faster resource creation
Rather than having to always manually enter all the data for a new contractor or piece of equipment, you can define those entries that you want to have pre-filled, by defining reusable resource types. For example you can define address, job code, time tracking, work schedule, activity checklist and more just once, and then use this to pre-fill your fields when creating a new resource. Find out more here:

  • Standardize Resource Creation with Reusable Resource Types in SAP Fieldglass

Tailored list views based on user roles
List views are core to daily operations for teams managing job postings, work orders, and statements of work. However, only some of the many columns shown are relevant to a user in specific role. Although users could customize their views, it turns out that this is rarely done. Now, customers can define views per role, specifying which columns to show and which to hide. This supports faster, more focused decisions and delivers a cleaner, more intuitive experience. Find out more:

  • Documentation: Customized List Views.

One-click line item copy for purchase orders
Here is a nice little detail that will make users smile: with one click, you can now copy the previous line item when creating a purchase order. In many procurement scenarios the same services are purchased regularly, so it is very helpful for users to copy an item and then make only the few necessary changes before continuing with the next item. As you can see in Figure 13, we have introduced a “copy” icon to duplicate the existing PO line.

Figure 13: One-click duplication of purchase order line. Alt Text: a screen snippet showing a PO line with the icon for copying the line on the left of the line.

Making it easier for suppliers to manage line items
We have introduced a number of improvements for suppliers working on SoWs (Statements of Work). For example, when purchasing contracts are in place, it makes sense to include information from the contract in a purchase order to ensure compliance, accurate pricing and so on. Navigating to the right information in the contract when creating a purchase order has been a challenge – but no longer! Suppliers can search and insert contract segments in any order now. Find out more:

  • Documentation: Supplier Workflow Enhancements on SOW Characteristics Tab.

Making it easier for suppliers to maintain charges against PO items
In asset-intensive and plant maintenance industries, suppliers frequently need to submit or resubmit charges against PO lines. This is especially common when working with complex SOWs that involve multiple tasks and services. Up until now, suppliers encountered multiple navigation paths when adding or correcting line items, particularly after a charge is rejected due to issues like incorrect pricing or missing details. Now, this has been made significantly easier: all relevant actions are accessible no matter where they begin the process. Figure 14 shows an example.

Figure 14: Supplier access to all actions when managing PO line charges. Alt Text: two screenshots, showing the card “Corrective maintenance repairs – Engineering Repairs Services and Parts” in different contexts, on the left with an “Edit” button, on the right with an “Itemize” button.

 

Further Information

For an overview of the major new features with the 2511 release, have a look at:

  • SAP Fieldglass 2025.11 Release Spotlight Features Guide.

These videos are available too:

  • SAP Fieldglass 2025.11 Release Highlights Video - Joule
  • SAP Fieldglass 2025.11 Release Highlights Video - Configuration Manager
  • SAP Fieldglass 2025.11 Release Highlights Video - General Updates
  • SAP Fieldglass 2025.11 Release Highlights Video - Services Procurement

Have a look at all the latest updates, month by month, in the

  • What’s New Viewer for SAP Fieldglass.
  • This blog post explains how to use the What’s New Viewer:
    SAP Fieldglass November 2025 Release Communication Now Available!

Another good place to stay up to date is the SAP Fieldglass page on SAP Community.

 

Continued soon…

I hope you enjoyed this sixth part, covering SAP Fieldglass.

I will post part seven next, covering latest UX innovations for spend management with SAP Ariba.

Part 1 of the series lists all the parts, and links to those parts that have already been published:

  • SAP UX Q1/2026 Update – Part 1: AI, Joule, SAP Build Work Zone, SAP Mobile Start.

Do keep posting your experiences and recommendations yourself in the SAP Community, with the SAP Fiori and/or the User Experience tag! In case you are wondering how to get a list of the most recent blog posts on SAP Fiori and User Experience in our new SAP Community, use these links:

  • Latest blog posts on SAP Fiori in the SAP Community.
  • Latest blog posts on User Experience in the SAP Community.

For general information on design at SAP and SAP Fiori, check out:

  • www.sap.com/design.
  • www.sap.com/fiori    
  • SAP Community: SAP Fiori topic page.

entry.title: Re: Asset
entry.content:
HI ,

You have a NEW BAdI Implementation (created recently)

If you created a new implementation of one of the BAdIs:

  • No action is required.
  • SAP confirms that in such cases the message appears but:
    • It is not caused by an upgrade
    • You can hide / suppress the message

➟ Fix

Go to:
SPRO → Asset Accounting → Transactions → Maintain Message Types
and suppress Message AU176 at the required message level (warning/info).

 

see sap note 3297821 .

entry.title: CDS view for BP Tax number
entry.content:
How to access Business Partner Tax Number via CDS views


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: CDS view for BP Tax number
entry.content:
The BP Tax Number field stores the value in table DFKKBPTAXNUM. CDS View I_BUSINESSPARTNERTAXNUMBER fetches data from table DFKKBPTAXNUM.

CDS Views I_CUSTOMER and I_SUPPLIER can also be used to get the tax numbers. These CDS views provide fields TaxNumber1, 2, ..., 5 which are filled with the values inserted in tax categories. There is also a field in these CDS Views for VATRegistration.

The steps to maintain tax numbers with Tax Categories in App Maintain Business Partner are as follows:

  1. Go to App Maintain Business Partner.
  2. Create or Open an existing BP in Business Partner (Gen.) role.
  3. Go to Identification Tab.
  4. Go to section Tax Numbers to maintain tax categories and its correspondng tax number.

Note: If the Tax Number maintained is for a country which is different than the BP country maintained, then KNA1 and LFA1 tables would not store tax numbers. Hence, in such scenarios, CDS Views I_CUSTOMER and I_SUPPLIER would return blank values

entry.title: Re: SAP Databricks – Best Practices SQL Query Performance Tuning Tips
entry.content:
@N1kh1l - In a Zero‑Copy integration model, SAP Datasphere federates or shares data directly from SAP or non‑SAP systems (including Databricks Delta Lake) without moving or duplicating it. Hence Datasphere(DSP) manages logical partitioning and data organization at the semantic layer.


SAP Databricks does support Liquid Clustering, because SAP Databricks is a fully embedded OEM version of the Databricks platform inside SAP Business Data Cloud, and it uses the same Delta Lake engine that Liquid Clustering is built on.

entry.title: Is SAP ByD supporting CXMAL scenarios
entry.content:
Yes — you can use SAP Business ByDesign (ByD) to send e‑invoices to GEP SMART, but only if you generate and send them in cXML, because GEP SMART’s preferred direct‑connect integration format for invoices is cXML.

Below is a clear breakdown using the evidence collected:

  1. Does GEP SMART accept cXML invoices?

Yes.
Multiple GEP documents explicitly state that GEP SMART supports cXML for B2B invoice integration:

  • ConocoPhillips' GEP SMART integration guidelines confirm that GEP integrates suppliers for PO & invoice handling through cXML/PIDX, and that the preferred method for invoicing is cXML.

  • GEP’s own SAP Adapter documentation says it can convert SAP ERP transactional data into cXML and upload it to GEP SMART.
    [gep.com]

So the receiving side (GEP SMART) absolutely supports cXML invoices.

  1. Can SAP Business ByDesign send cXML invoices?

SAP Business ByDesign does not natively output cXML for customer invoices, but you can send cXML in two supported ways:

Option A — Integrate via SAP Business Network (Ariba-style cXML routing)

SAP Business Network explicitly supports cXML as an outbound routing method for invoices:

  • You can set the routing method for invoices to cXML, which means SAP ByD → SAP Business Network → Buyer (GEP SMART)*.

This requires:
✔ A Business Network (Ariba) supplier account
✔ cXML InvoiceDetailRequest mapping
✔ HTTPS endpoint configuration

If the buyer lets you connect through SAP Business Network to GEP SMART, this can work smoothly.

Option B — Use a middleware that generates cXML (recommended)

SAP ByD can send invoice data via SOAP/XML outbound services. This data can then be transformed into cXML by:

  • SAP CPI (Cloud Platform Integration)
  • A partner solution (e.g., sinsou cXML integration)

  • GEP SMART SAP Adapter (but this is for SAP ERP, not SAP ByD)

SAP ByD supports external invoice integration via electronic data exchange channels, so the architecture looks like:

SAP ByD → outbound SOAP/XML → middleware → cXML → GEP SMART (Direct Connect)

This is the most common setup for ByD + GEP integrations.

  1. Important Clarification

There is no direct native export of cXML from SAP Business ByDesign (unlike SAP ERP + GEP Adapter).
Therefore:

➤ Direct ByD → GEP SMART in cXML is not possible without middleware.

But

➤ ByD → Middleware → cXML → GEP SMART works and is fully supported.

  1. Final Answer

Yes, you can send e‑invoices from SAP Business ByDesign to GEP SMART in cXML format, but you must use either SAP Business Network or an integration/middleware layer to convert and deliver the cXML file, because ByD does not generate cXML natively.

GEP SMART accepts cXML invoices, so as long as your integration produces valid cXML, the connection is supported.
